spec.title: Brand Identity & Marketing Asset Package for Growing PC Company
spec.grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the Upwork brief in db_all.json): client is "Tech World PC", a growing custom-computer company (gaming PCs, workstations, refurbished systems, repair) selling on eBay/marketplaces, wanting a scalable professional brand identity. The selected logo direction is REAL: "Geometric hexagon icon, integrated 'TW' monogram, clean corporate styling, strong readability at small sizes," with the icon required to work independently for watermarks, social profiles, product photos, stickers, and packaging. The style words (Professional, Modern, Trustworthy, Technical, Premium, Scalable) and reference brands (Intel, Corsair, Lenovo, ASUS, EVGA) and the "do NOT want" list (no esports/mascots/cartoon/Twitch/aggressive gaming-team logos) are REAL. The supplied AI-generated concept artwork is REAL ("We have AI-generated concept artwork used during the brainstorming phase"). The deliverable list is REAL and verbatim: logo package (Primary Horizontal, Stacked, Icon-Only, Light-BG, Dark-BG, Single-Color, Favicon) in AI/SVG/EPS/PDF/PNG at 512/1024/2048/4096px transparent; a concise brand guide (logo usage, color palette, typography, spacing, background usage); marketplace assets (eBay store banner, listing watermark, "Play the Latest Games" promotional banner); social assets (Facebook cover, YouTube banner, profile picture); business assets (business card, warranty card, shipping insert, email signature). ENRICHED (added only where the brief genuinely implies a long-horizon connector workflow, NOT new scope): I made the deliverable concrete by assigning exact connector-renderable specs (pixel dims, transparent PNG exports, SVG vectorization), I added an animated logo sting for social use (the brief explicitly lists YouTube + social profiles + "Play the Latest Games" banner, so a short motion logo for video intros is an honest extension of "social media assets"), and I added a Firefly concept board to "assemble the logo + colorway + template renders into a deliverable concept board" — which operationalizes the brief's requirement to present "a cohesive brand system" and "background usage examples." The brief's explicit anti-trace clause ("we are NOT looking for someone to simply trace AI images... rebuild from scratch using professional vector techniques") is honored: image_vectorize is used to recover a CLEAN editable vector starting point from the rough concept, which the spec then requires to be refined into a true geometric hexagon/TW monogram — not a raw auto-trace dump. No client, brand name, service list, or reference brand was invented.
spec.one_line_ask: Build Tech World PC a production-ready brand identity system from their rough AI concept logo: vectorize and refine it into a clean hexagon/TW-monogram mark with light/dark/single-color/favicon variants, then populate Adobe Express templates for the business card, eBay store banner, "Play the Latest Games" promo banner, Facebook cover and YouTube banner with the brand name and taglines, produce a short animated logo sting for social/video, and assemble the logo + colorway + all template renders into a single Firefly concept board for client sign-off.
spec.full_brief:
Tech World PC is a growing custom-computer company (custom gaming PCs, workstations, refurbished systems, computer repair, upgrades and components) that currently sells primarily through eBay and other online marketplaces and wants to establish a professional, consistent brand identity that scales with the business. They have already completed the concept phase and selected a logo DIRECTION — a geometric hexagon icon with an integrated "TW" monogram, clean corporate styling, and strong readability at small sizes — but the only artifact they have is rough AI-generated concept artwork from brainstorming. They are explicit that they do NOT want a blind trace of the AI image: they want the concept recovered into clean professional vector form and refined into an original, scalable mark. The icon must function independently from the wordmark so it can live in watermarks, social profile pictures, product photos, stickers and future packaging. The brand must feel like a professional technology company in the spirit of Intel, Corsair, Lenovo, ASUS and EVGA — Professional, Modern, Trustworthy, Technical, Premium, Scalable — and must AVOID esports branding, mascots, cartoon characters, Twitch-style graphics and aggressive gaming-team logos.

The deliverable is a complete brand package, not a logo-only project. (1) LOGO SYSTEM: from the supplied concept, produce a clean vector mark and its required lockups — Primary Horizontal, Stacked, Icon-Only, Light-Background version, Dark-Background version, Single-Color version, and a Favicon — exported as transparent PNGs at 512/1024/2048/4096px plus the editable vector (SVG). (2) BRAND CORE: a concise brand-guide concept covering logo usage, color palette (a primary "tech blue" plus a dark slate and a neutral, in the Intel/Lenovo family — NOT neon esports color), typography recommendation, spacing/clear-space, and background-usage examples. (3) MARKETING & SOCIAL ASSETS built on Adobe Express templates and filled with the real brand name and taglines: an eBay store banner, a listing watermark (the Icon-Only mark over product photos), a "Play the Latest Games" promotional banner, a Facebook cover, a YouTube banner, and a profile-picture crop. (4) MOTION: a short animated logo sting (the hexagon assembling / TW reveal) for use as a social and YouTube intro. (5) PRESENTATION: every render — the refined mark, the colorway, and all populated templates — assembled into one Firefly concept board the client can review as the "cohesive brand system" before final source-file handoff.

Hard constraints the agent must respect: the mark must be derived from the SUPPLIED concept (consistent hexagon + TW monogram), not invented from scratch; all logo PNG exports must be on a TRANSPARENT background at the four exact sizes (512/1024/2048/4096); the single-color and dark-background versions must be legible (no color-on-color illegibility); the brand name "Tech World PC" and exactly the listed taglines must appear correctly spelled across every template (text-only fills — the template layouts and the chosen corporate font are not to be visually broken); the palette and styling must read corporate-tech (Intel/Corsair/Lenovo family) with zero esports/mascot/Twitch cues; the animated sting must use the SAME refined mark (no re-drawn or off-brand logo); and the final concept board must contain every asset so a single deep-link presents the whole system.

inputs.name: techworldpc_concept_logo_raw.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A rough, slightly messy AI-brainstorm logo concept for a custom-PC company called 'Tech World PC'. A geometric hexagon icon enclosing an interlocking 'TW' monogram, rendered in corporate tech-blue (#1F6FEB) with a darker slate accent, sitting on a plain off-white background. Make it look like an unrefined first-pass generative concept: very slightly uneven hexagon edges, soft anti-aliased raster edges, a faint drop shadow, minor color banding in the gradient, the monogram strokes not perfectly aligned, a couple of stray semi-transparent pixels around the mark. Clean corporate styling overall (NOT esports, no mascot, no cartoon, no flames, no Twitch glow), readable, but clearly a raster concept that needs to be rebuilt as clean vector. No text other than the TW monogram inside the hexagon. 2048x2048, centered, plenty of margin.
inputs.realism_notes: This is the client's REAL supplied artifact: 'AI-generated concept artwork used during the brainstorming phase.' It must look like an imperfect raster concept (soft edges, slight banding, stray pixels) so that image_vectorize has something real to clean up and the anti-trace refinement step is meaningful. Tech-blue + slate palette keeps it in the Intel/Lenovo family per the brief; deliberately NOT neon/esports.

inputs.name: techworldpc_product_photo.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean, well-lit studio product photograph of a custom gaming PC tower: matte black mid-tower case with a tempered-glass side panel, tasteful blue interior accent lighting (subtle, NOT rainbow RGB), visible graphics card and cooling, photographed three-quarter front on a seamless light-grey studio backdrop with a soft reflection on the surface. Realistic product photography: accurate metal and glass reflections, real dust-free but physically plausible surfaces, natural soft shadow, correct perspective, slight depth-of-field falloff, no text, no logos on the case, no watermark. Premium-but-approachable, in the spirit of a Corsair/Lenovo marketplace listing photo. 2000x1500, landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Marketplace listing photo onto which the Icon-Only mark is applied as a watermark, and which seeds the eBay/promo banners. Subtle blue accent (not rainbow RGB) keeps it corporate per the 'do NOT want esports' constraint. Real studio reflections/shadow so the watermark composite reads as a genuine listing image.

inputs.name: techworldpc_brand_brief.json
inputs.gen_prompt: Produce a compact JSON brand brief for 'Tech World PC' with keys: brand_name ('Tech World PC'), tagline_primary ('Built to Perform'), tagline_promo ('Play the Latest Games'), tagline_marketplace ('Custom Gaming PCs · Workstations · Repair'), palette ({primary_hex:'#1F6FEB', dark_hex:'#0B1F3A', neutral_hex:'#E8EDF4', accent_hex:'#1F6FEB'}), font_primary_role ('geometric/humanist sans, corporate-tech, e.g. Inter/Eurostile family'), reference_brands (['Intel','Corsair','Lenovo','ASUS','EVGA']), avoid (['esports','mascots','cartoon','Twitch-style','aggressive gaming-team logos']), template_copy ({business_card:{name_line:'Tech World PC', role_line:'Custom Gaming PCs · Workstations · Repair', contact:'support@techworldpc.com · techworldpc.com'}, ebay_banner:{headline:'Tech World PC', sub:'Custom Gaming PCs · Workstations · Refurbished'}, promo_banner:{headline:'Play the Latest Games', sub:'Custom-Built by Tech World PC'}, facebook_cover:{headline:'Tech World PC', sub:'Built to Perform'}, youtube_banner:{headline:'Tech World PC', sub:'Custom PCs · Workstations · Repair'}}). Valid JSON only.
inputs.realism_notes: The single source of truth for every fill_text and document_render_layout call — guarantees the brand name and exact taglines (including the brief's literal 'Play the Latest Games') are spelled identically everywhere. Palette stays in the Intel/Lenovo corporate-tech family the brief demands. Data input, so gemini-2.5-flash per section D.

outputs.name: techworldpc_logo_master.svg
outputs.spec: Clean editable SVG of the refined hexagon + interlocking 'TW' monogram, single combined path set, transparent background, corporate tech-blue (#1F6FEB) on the color version; geometry derived from the supplied concept but rebuilt clean (no soft raster edges, no stray pixels).
outputs.rubric_hook: Is the SVG a clean vector (no embedded raster, no auto-trace garbage paths), faithful to the supplied hexagon/TW concept, and legible at favicon size? Penalize a blind raster-embed or an off-concept redraw.

outputs.name: techworldpc_logo_variants_PNG_set
outputs.spec: Transparent-background PNG exports of the Icon-Only, Light-BG, Dark-BG and Single-Color lockups, each at 512, 1024, 2048 and 4096 px (square), exact sizes, alpha preserved.
outputs.rubric_hook: Are all four exact sizes present per variant, truly transparent, and is the dark-BG/single-color version legible (correct contrast, no color-on-color)? Penalize missing sizes, baked-in backgrounds, or illegible variants.

outputs.name: techworldpc_business_card.png
outputs.spec: Express business-card template populated with the brand name, role line and contact, 1050x600 px (3.5x2in @300dpi bleed-trimmed) PNG, corporate-tech styling.
outputs.rubric_hook: Brand name + contact correctly spelled, template layout intact, font/styling unchanged by the text fill, on-brand palette.

outputs.name: techworldpc_ebay_store_banner.png
outputs.spec: eBay store banner from an Express template, 1200x270 px PNG, headline 'Tech World PC' + sub line, refined logo placed.
outputs.rubric_hook: Correct eBay banner dimensions, brand name/sub present, logo on-brand and not distorted.

outputs.name: techworldpc_play_latest_games_banner.png
outputs.spec: Promotional banner from an Express template, 1500x500 px PNG, headline exactly 'Play the Latest Games' + 'Custom-Built by Tech World PC', product photo + logo.
outputs.rubric_hook: Exact 'Play the Latest Games' wording (the brief's literal asset), product image composited, on-brand, no esports cues.

outputs.name: techworldpc_facebook_cover.png
outputs.spec: Facebook cover from an Express template, 1640x624 px PNG, brand name + 'Built to Perform'.
outputs.rubric_hook: Correct FB cover size, safe-area respected, brand name/tagline correct, on-brand.

outputs.name: techworldpc_youtube_banner.png
outputs.spec: YouTube channel banner from an Express template, 2560x1440 px PNG (with 1546x423 safe area), brand name + services sub.
outputs.rubric_hook: Correct YT banner size, content inside safe area, brand name correct, on-brand.

outputs.name: techworldpc_profile_picture.png
outputs.spec: Icon-Only profile-picture crop, 800x800 px PNG square, Light-BG version, centered with clear space.
outputs.rubric_hook: Square, centered icon, correct clear space, reads at small size.

outputs.name: techworldpc_listing_watermark_demo.png
outputs.spec: Product photo with the Icon-Only mark applied as a semi-transparent corner watermark, 2000x1500 px PNG.
outputs.rubric_hook: Watermark uses the refined Icon-Only mark, legible but unobtrusive, correctly placed, not distorting the product photo.

outputs.name: techworldpc_logo_sting.mp4
outputs.spec: Short animated logo sting, ~3-4s, square 1080x1080 (and the design exportable to 1920x1080), hexagon assembling into the TW reveal, transparent-friendly export, uses the SAME refined mark.
outputs.rubric_hook: Motion built from the actual refined logo (not a redraw), on-brand corporate motion (no flashy esports glitch), clean reveal, correct duration/size.

outputs.name: techworldpc_concept_board
outputs.spec: A single Firefly Board deep-link assembling the refined mark, the logo variant set, the colorway swatches, the animated sting and all populated templates as one reviewable concept board.
outputs.rubric_hook: Board contains EVERY asset (logo system + colorway + all templates + sting), is a working deep-link, and presents a coherent 'cohesive brand system'.

connector_workflow.output: upload session + presigned target for the raw concept logo
connector_workflow.note: Begin pushing the client's supplied AI concept artwork into CC storage so connector tools can reference it by URN.
Finalize the upload; now the rough concept exists as a stable URN reused downstream.

connector_workflow.output: concept_logo_cutout.png (mark isolated on transparency, stray-pixel background dropped)
connector_workflow.note: Clean the supplied raster: cut the mark off its off-white concept background and strip the soft halo before vectorizing, so the trace is crisp.

connector_workflow.output: techworldpc_logo_master.svg (clean editable vector of the hexagon + TW monogram)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Recover a CLEAN vector from the de-backgrounded concept — the basis for refinement into the production mark (honors the brief's 'rebuild, do not blind-trace' clause).
Set up storage so the master SVG, variant exports and template renders are organized for handoff and board assembly.

connector_workflow.output: logo_singlecolor + logo_darkbg recolors (single-color #0B1F3A and white-on-dark variants of the master mark)
connector_workflow.note: Derive the Single-Color and Dark-Background lockups from the one master vector using the palette in the brand brief — keeps every variant on-brand from a single source.
Render the master + recolored marks to the exact required transparent PNG sizes and the 800x800 profile crop — deterministic spec-exact export of the logo system.

connector_workflow.output: chosen Express templates: business card, eBay store banner, promo banner, Facebook cover, YouTube banner
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Find on-brand corporate-tech Express templates at the right dimensions for each marketing/social deliverable (no esports styling).

connector_workflow.output: recommended corporate-tech sans (Inter/Eurostile-family) for the brand guide typography section
connector_workflow.note: Satisfy the brief's 'typography recommendations' guideline and lock a consistent font choice referenced across the templates.
MUST-EXERCISE. Pour the exact brand name / role / contact and the 'Built to Perform' tagline into the card and FB cover templates (text-only, layout preserved).
MUST-EXERCISE (2nd fill pass). Fill the marketplace + YouTube + promo headlines, including the literal 'Play the Latest Games' wording from the brief.
Bring the studio product photo into CC storage for the watermark and promo-banner composite.
Finalize; product photo now referenceable by URN for compositing steps.

connector_workflow.output: techworldpc_listing_watermark_demo.png (Icon-Only mark placed as corner watermark over the product photo)
connector_workflow.note: Composite the refined Icon-Only mark onto the real product photo to demonstrate the required listing watermark use-case.
Lay out the 'Play the Latest Games' promo banner deterministically: product photo, refined logo, exact headline at the required dimensions.

connector_workflow.output: techworldpc_brand_guide.pdf (logo usage, color palette swatches, typography, clear-space, background usage)
connector_workflow.note: Render the concise brand-guide concept the brief requires, pulling the recommended font, palette swatches and the logo variants into one spec sheet.
MUST-EXERCISE. Animate the SAME refined master mark into a short corporate logo sting for social/YouTube intros (no off-brand redraw, no esports glitch).

connector_workflow.output: verified previews of every render (logo set, all templates, watermark, brand guide, sting)
connector_workflow.note: State-tracking checkpoint: preview each produced asset to confirm names/sizes/spelling before board assembly — catches a missing size or a mis-spelled headline.

connector_workflow.output: techworldpc_concept_board (Firefly Board deep-link with the full system)
connector_workflow.note: MUST-EXERCISE. Assemble the refined mark, variant set, colorway, all populated templates, watermark demo, brand guide and animated sting into one reviewable concept board for client sign-off.

spec.iterative_chaining_note: The chain is genuinely output→input throughout. The client's rough concept (techworldpc_concept_logo_raw.png) is uploaded and finalized into a URN (steps 1→2), de-backgrounded (3), then vectorized into the single master SVG (4) that EVERY downstream logo asset descends from: image_apply_color_overlay (6) recolors that master into the single-color and dark-bg lockups; document_render_vector (7) renders the master + recolors into the exact-size transparent PNG set and the profile crop; image_fill_area (14) composites the rendered Icon-Only mark onto the uploaded product photo (12→13) for the watermark; document_render_layout (15) pulls the same product URN + rendered logo + the step-11 promo text into the 'Play the Latest Games' banner; and animate_design (17) animates the SAME master SVG into the sting. The brand-brief JSON is the cross-step text spine, feeding font_recommend (9), both fill_text passes (10, 11), and both layout renders (15, 16) so the brand name and the literal 'Play the Latest Games' tagline are spelled identically everywhere. search_design (8) feeds both fill_text passes. asset_inline_preview (18) reads back all renders for a verification checkpoint, and create_firefly_board (19) ingests the union of steps 4, 6, 7, 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, 17 — i.e. the entire produced system — into one deliverable board. Nothing is a dead-end: each producing step is consumed by a later one, and the master SVG alone fans out to five distinct downstream branches.
spec.difficulty_rationale: HARD on every axis the flagship bar cares about. (1) Long horizon: 19 dependent connector calls across three tool groups (express template track + photo/raster + asset storage), with the must-exercise tools image_vectorize, search_design, fill_text (twice), animate_design and create_firefly_board all on the critical path. (2) Heavy state tracking: one master SVG fans out into five downstream branches (recolors, exact-size renders, watermark composite, promo layout, animation) — the agent must keep a single canonical mark and not let variants drift; two separate uploaded URNs (concept logo, product photo) must each be referenced by the right later steps. (3) Exact-spec pressure: four logo variants × four exact pixel sizes (512/1024/2048/4096) all on true transparency, plus five platform templates each at its own non-negotiable dimension (1050x600, 1200x270, 1500x500, 1640x624, 2560x1440 with safe areas) — a single wrong size or a baked-in background fails the rubric. (4) Brand-fidelity traps: the brief explicitly forbids esports/mascot/Twitch/neon styling and demands an Intel/Corsair/Lenovo corporate feel, and explicitly forbids a blind AI-trace — so the agent must both vectorize AND demonstrate refinement, and must keep the palette/motion corporate. (5) Exact-string discipline: the brand name and the literal 'Play the Latest Games' headline must be spelled correctly across every fill_text and layout call, driven from a shared JSON, with a preview checkpoint (step 18) that an honest agent must actually use to catch a missing export size or a typo before the final board. (6) Assembly: the closing create_firefly_board must contain EVERY produced asset, so any earlier omission surfaces at the end.

issues: TOOL MISUSE (step 14): image_fill_area is GENERATIVE FILL (invents/replaces content inside a selection per sheet A1), not a compositor. It cannot place a SPECIFIC known Icon-Only mark as a semi-transparent corner watermark at a set position/opacity. No logo-overlay/compositing tool exists in the sheet, so the techworldpc_listing_watermark_demo.png deliverable is not feasible as routed.

TYPE/CAPABILITY MISMATCH (step 6): image_apply_color_overlay is a RASTER tool (sheet section A1, Lightroom-grade pixel op) but is fed the VECTOR SVG from step 4. Also a single flat color-overlay cannot produce a 'white-on-dark' lockup, which needs two operations (recolor the mark to white PLUS add a dark background). The Dark-BG variant is therefore not producible by this step.

EXPRESS-ONLY VIOLATION (step 17): The sheet states animate_design is 'Express designs only' and does preset motion (fade/slide/zoom). It is fed a raw vectorized SVG/PNG (steps 4/7), which is NOT an Express design, and the requested 'hexagon assembles -> TW reveal' is a bespoke build-on keyframe animation that exceeds preset fade/slide/zoom. The animated sting is overstated and mis-routed.

ANTI-TRACE REQUIREMENT UNSATISFIABLE: image_vectorize (step 4) is auto-trace (raster->SVG). The sheet has NO vector path-editing/refinement tool, yet the spec and the SVG rubric_hook demand a 'rebuilt from scratch, refined, NOT a blind trace' mark and penalize 'auto-trace garbage paths.' The workflow contains no refinement step, so the headline anti-trace constraint cannot be met by any listed tool.

MISSING EXPORT STEP: fill_text is TEXT-ONLY and does not export an image (sheet A3; the sheet's own chain is search_design -> fill_text -> animate_design -> EXPORT). Steps 10/11 nonetheless treat fill_text outputs directly as final PNGs (techworldpc_business_card.png, _facebook_cover.png, _ebay_store_banner.png, _youtube_banner.png). No export/render step is present to turn the filled templates into the deliverable PNGs at their exact dimensions.

MINOR PLUMBING GAP: steps 1->2 and 12->13 go straight from asset_initialize_file_upload to asset_finalize_file_upload, skipping asset_get_presigned_urls / the presigned PUT that uploads the actual bytes.
fixes: Replace step 14 (image_fill_area) for the watermark with a real composite path: render the Icon-Only mark over the product photo via document_render_layout (text+images layout placing the logo image at corner with reduced opacity), or build the watermark as an Express design and export it. Do not use generative fill to place a known logo.

Rework step 6: do the recolors at render time instead of via a raster overlay on a vector. Use document_render_vector with the master SVG re-styled to single-color (#0B1F3A) and to white, and render the Dark-BG variant as white-mark-on-dark-canvas (mark + dark background composited in document_render_layout). Drop image_apply_color_overlay as the vector recolor mechanism.

Re-route step 17: first place the master mark into an Express design (search_design/an Express design doc), THEN call animate_design, and downgrade the description to the preset motion animate_design actually supports (fade/slide/zoom reveal) rather than a custom 'hexagon assembling' keyframe build, OR move the bespoke sting out of scope.

Add an explicit refinement acknowledgment: since no path-editing tool exists, either soften the spec/rubric to accept a clean image_vectorize output as the 'rebuilt vector' (and stop penalizing it as a 'trace'), or note the refinement is a human/source-file step outside the connector set. As written the anti-trace requirement cannot be graded as feasible.

Insert explicit export/render steps after each fill_text: e.g. document_render_layout or an Express export call producing the business card (1050x600), eBay banner (1200x270), FB cover (1640x624), and YouTube banner (2560x1440) PNGs at their exact required dimensions; fill_text alone does not emit these files.
Add asset_get_presigned_urls (and the presigned PUT) between asset_initialize_file_upload and asset_finalize_file_upload for both the concept logo (1->2) and product photo (12->13) uploads.
